subject: Human Resources
title: Workplace Violence Prevention
description: A micro-lesson that makes Workplace Violence Prevention usable.

hook.text: Two million American workers report being victims of workplace violence annually. It's not rare—it's an epidemic.

buildup.text: Workplace violence ranges from threats and intimidation to physical assaults—from coworkers, customers, or outsiders.

discovery.text: OSHA's General Duty Clause requires employers to maintain a workplace free from recognized serious hazards—including violence.

climax.text: Prevention programs include threat assessment teams, zero-tolerance policies, training, and secure facility design.

punchline.text: Violence at work isn't random. Ignoring the warning signs is a choice.

quiz.question: What does OSHA's General Duty Clause require regarding workplace violence?
options: Employers must maintain workplaces free from recognized serious hazards
Employers have no responsibility for violence
Only law enforcement handles workplace violence
